title: Anatomy and Physiology 2e
excerpt: A comprehensive, peer-reviewed open textbook covering human anatomy and physiology organized by body systems, designed for two-semester courses in life sciences and allied health.
keyIdeas: Structure and function are inseparable at every level of biological organization
Homeostasis is the central organizing principle of human physiology
The body operates as an integrated network of interacting organ systems
Anatomical terminology provides a universal language for describing the human body
keyTakeaways: Master anatomical position, directional terms, and body planes to communicate precisely about the human body
Understand how feedback mechanisms maintain homeostasis across all organ systems
Recognize the interdependence of body systems in health and disease
Apply structure-function relationships to predict physiological responses
whoShouldRead: Pre-nursing and allied health students starting their anatomy & physiology sequence
Biology undergraduates seeking a free, peer-reviewed A&P reference
Self-learners and career-changers entering healthcare fields
Instructors looking for an adaptable OER textbook for two-semester A&P courses
whoShouldSkip: Advanced medical students needing surgical or cadaver-level anatomical detail
Readers seeking entertainment rather than systematic textbook study
Professionals requiring in-depth physiology research with extensive molecular mechanisms
